#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <vector>
#include <string>
#include <cstdint>

struct NetworkPacket {
    uint32_t packet_id;
    std::string username;
    std::vector<uint32_t> scores;
    std::string metadata;
    
    void serialise(auto& stream) {
        stream << packet_id;
        stream << static_cast<uint32_t>(username.size());
        stream << username;
        stream << static_cast<uint32_t>(scores.size());
        stream << scores;
        stream << null_terminated(metadata);
    }
};

std::vector<uint8_t> serialize_packet(const NetworkPacket& packet) {
    hexi::stream_adaptor<std::vector<uint8_t>> stream;
    packet.serialise(stream);
    return stream.str();
}
```</answer>

#include <cstdint>
#include <span>

struct MessageHeader {
    uint16_t message_type;
    uint32_t body_length;
    uint32_t footer;

    return header;

struct DataResult {
    uint8_t data_type;
    std::vector<uint32_t> int_items;
    std::vector<float> float_items;
};

DataResult preview_and_read_data(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    hexi::buffer_adaptor adaptor(buffer);
    hexi::binary_stream stream(adaptor);
    
    uint8_t data_type = 0;
    if (!adaptor.peek(&data_type, sizeof(data_type))) {
        return DataResult{0, {}, {}};
    }
    
    // Check if buffer has enough data for header (data_type + item_count)
    if (adaptor.size() < sizeof(data_type) + sizeof(uint32_t)) {
        return DataResult{0, {}, {}};
    }
    
    uint32_t item_count = 0;
    if (!stream.read(&item_count, sizeof(item_count))) {
        return DataResult{0, {}, {}};
    }
    
    // Calculate expected total size and check availability
    uint64_t expected_size = sizeof(data_type) + sizeof(uint32_t) + 
                           static_cast<uint64_t>(item_count) * 4;
    if (adaptor.size() < expected_size) {
        return DataResult{0, {}, {}};
    }
    
    std::vector<uint32_t> int_items;
    std::vector<float> float_items;
    
    if (data_type == 0) { // integers
        int_items.resize(item_count);
        if (!stream.read(int_items.data(), item_count * sizeof(uint32_t))) {
            return DataResult{0, {}, {}};
        }
    } else if (data_type == 1) { // floats
        float_items.resize(item_count);
        if (!stream.read(float_items.data(), item_count * sizeof(float))) {
            return DataResult{0, {}, {}};
        }
    }
    
    return DataResult{data_type, std::move(int_items), std::move(float_items)};
}
```</answer>

#include <cstdint>

template<typename T>

    return hexi::endian::native_to_big(value);
}

template<typename T>

    return hexi::endian::big_to_native(value);
}

template<typename T>

    return hexi::endian::native_to_little(value);
}

template<typename T>

    return hexi::endian::little_to_native(value);
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/endian.h>
#include <cstdint>
#include <bit>

template<typename T>
void convert_to_big_endian_inplace(T& value) {
    value = hexi::native_to_big(value);
}

template<typename T>
void convert_to_little_endian_inplace(T& value) {
    value = hexi::native_to_little(value);
}

template<std::endian from, std::endian to, typename T>
void convert_endianness_inplace(T& value) {
    if constexpr (from != to) {
        if constexpr (from == std::endian::big) {
            value = hexi::big_to_native(value);
            if constexpr (to == std::endian::little) {
                value = hexi::native_to_little(value);
            }
        } else if constexpr (from == std::endian::little) {
            value = hexi::little_to_native(value);
            if constexpr (to == std::endian::big) {
                value = hexi::native_to_big(value);
            }
        } else if constexpr (from == std::endian::native) {
            if constexpr (to == std::endian::big) {
                value = hexi::native_to_big(value);
            } else if constexpr (to == std::endian::little) {
                value = hexi::native_to_little(value);
            }
        }
    }
}

First, the read_file_data function. The goal is to read a specified number of bytes into a destination buffer. So I'll open the file using file_buffer, then use read() to copy bytes into a vector. But wait, what if the file doesn't exist or there's an error? Maybe I should handle exceptions, but the problem statement might not require that. Let's assume basic error handling is okay here.

Next, preview_file_data. This should copy data without advancing the read pointer. Oh right, because if we just read normally, the pointer moves. So maybe we need to save the current position, read the data, then restore the position. Or perhaps there's a method in file_buffer that allows copying without advancing. Wait, maybe file_buffer has a peek() method? Or maybe we can read into a temporary buffer and then reset the position. Hmm. Alternatively, since file_buffer is a sequence container, maybe we can use seek() to go back after reading. Let me think. Suppose we read the data, then seek back the same number of bytes. <answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/static_buffer.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>
#include <algorithm>

template<size_t buffer_size = 1024>
void write_data_to_buffer(hexi::static_buffer<std::byte, buffer_size>& buffer, const std::vector<uint8_t>& data) {
    size_t current_size = buffer.size();
    size_t available_space = buffer.capacity() - current_size;
    size_t bytes_to_write = std::min(data.size(), available_space);
    
    if (bytes_to_write > 0) {
        std::copy_n(data.begin(), bytes_to_write, buffer.data() + current_size);
    }
}

template<size_t buffer_size = 1024>
std::vector<uint8_t> read_data_from_buffer(hexi::static_buffer<std::byte, buffer_size>& buffer, size_t bytes_to_read) {
    size_t current_size = buffer.size();
    size_t bytes_available = std::min(bytes_to_read, current_size);
    
    std::vector<uint8_t> result(bytes_available);
    std::copy_n(reinterpret_cast<const uint8_t*>(buffer.data()), bytes_available, result.begin());
    return result;
}
```</answer>

#include <hexi/static_buffer.h>

template<size_t buffer_size = 1024>
struct BufferInfo {
    size_t capacity;
    size_t current_size;
    size_t free_space;
};

BufferInfo<> get_buffer_info(const hexi::static_buffer<std::byte, 1024>& buffer) {
    BufferInfo<> info;
    info.capacity = buffer.capacity();  // Get the total capacity of the buffer
    info.current_size = buffer.size();  // Get the current readable data size
    info.free_space = info.capacity - info.current_size;  // Calculate available free space
    return info;
}
```</answer>

#include <hexi/pmc/stream_base.h>
#include <hexi/shared.h>

size_t get_stream_size(const hexi::pmc::stream_base& stream) {
    return stream.buffer_size();
}

bool is_stream_empty(const hexi::pmc::stream_base& stream) {
    return stream.buffer_size() == 0;
}

struct StreamInfo {
    hexi::stream_state state;
    size_t buffer_size;
    bool is_empty;
};

StreamInfo get_stream_state_info(const hexi::pmc::stream_base& stream) {
    StreamInfo info;
    info.state = stream.state();
    info.buffer_size = stream.buffer_size();
    info.is_empty = (info.buffer_size == 0);
    return info;
}
```

This solution correctly uses the `stream_base` interface from the Hexi library to retrieve the necessary information about the stream's buffer and state. Each function performs its intended operation efficiently by leveraging the existing methods provided by the library.</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/pmc/binary_stream_writer.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/binary_stream_reader.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_adaptor.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

struct UserData {
    uint32_t user_id;
    std::string name;
    
    void serialise(auto& stream) {
        stream << user_id << name;
    }
};

void serialize_object(hexi::pmc::binary_stream_writer& writer, const UserData& data) {
    data.serialise(writer);
}

void deserialize_object(hexi::pmc::binary_stream_reader& reader, UserData& data) {
    data.serialise(reader);
}
```

**Explanation:**
- The `serialize_object` function uses the `serialise` method of `UserData` to write the object's data to the binary stream.
- The `deserialize_object` function similarly uses the `serialise` method, but this time with a `binary_stream_reader` to read data from the stream into the object.
- The `auto&` template parameter in the `serialise` method allows it to accept either a `binary_stream_writer` or `binary_stream_reader`, enabling bidirectional serialization.</answer>

#include <hexi/pmc/binary_stream_writer.h>

void write_string_to_stream(hexi::pmc::binary_stream_writer& writer, const std::string& str) {
    uint32_t len = static_cast<uint32_t>(str.size());
    writer.write(len);

}

    reader.read(str.data(), len);
    return str;
}

The task is to write a vector of uint32_t to a binary stream with a prefixed count. So, the steps would be: first write the size of the vector as a 32-bit integer, then write each element in the vector.

Wait, how does the Hexi library handle writing integers? I remember that binary_stream_writer has a write method that can take different data types. So for the count, I can use writer.write<uint32_t>(container.size()). Then loop through each element in the container and write them one by one using writer.write<uint32_t>(element).

For the second function, read_container_from_stream, we need to read the count first, then read that many elements. So first, read the count by using reader.read<uint32_t>(), then create a vector of that size. Then loop through each index, read each element, and assign it to the vector.

But wait, what if the stream doesn't have enough data? The problem statement probably assumes that the stream has the correct amount of data, so we don't need error handling here. So just proceed under normal circumstances.

Let me check the Hexi documentation. The binary_stream_writer and reader have methods like write and read that can handle fundamental types. So yes, using write<uint32_t> and read<uint32_t> should work.

So putting it all together:

For write_container_to_stream:
- Get the size of the container.
- Write the size as a uint32_t.
- Iterate over each element and write them.

- Read the count as uint32_t.

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/pmc/binary_stream_writer.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/binary_stream_reader.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_adaptor.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

void write_container_to_stream(hexi::pmc::binary_stream_writer& writer, const std::vector<uint32_t>& container) {
    // Write the count of elements as a 32-bit integer
    writer.write<uint32_t>(container.size());
    
    // Write each element in the container
    for (const auto& element : container) {
        writer.write<uint32_t>(element);
    }
}

std::vector<uint32_t> read_container_from_stream(hexi::pmc::binary_stream_reader& reader) {
    // Read the count of elements
    uint32_t count = reader.read<uint32_t>();
    
    // Create a vector of the appropriate size
    std::vector<uint32_t> container(count);
    
    // Read each element into the vector
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < count; ++i) {
        container[i] = reader.read<uint32_t>();
    }
    
    return container;
}
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_adaptor.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

void write_data_to_buffer(hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer, uint32_t value, const void* data, std::size_t length) {
    // Write the integer value to the buffer
    buffer.write(reinterpret_cast<const std::byte*>(&value), sizeof(value));
    // Write the byte array to the buffer
    buffer.write(static_cast<const std::byte*>(data), length);
}

void read_data_from_buffer(hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer, uint32_t& value, void* data, std::size_t length) {
    // Read the integer value from the buffer
    buffer.read(reinterpret_cast<std::byte*>(&value), sizeof(value));
    // Read the byte array from the buffer
    buffer.read(static_cast<std::byte*>(data), length);
}

struct BufferInfo {
    std::size_t size;
    std::size_t free_space;
};

BufferInfo get_buffer_info(hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer) {
    BufferInfo info;
    info.size = buffer.size();
    info.free_space = buffer.free();
    return info;
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_adaptor.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_read.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

std::size_t find_byte_in_buffer(hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer, std::byte value) {
    for (std::size_t i = 0; i < buffer.size(); ++i) {
        if (buffer[i] == value) {
            return i;
        }
    }
    return hexi::pmc::buffer_read<std::vector<std::byte>>::npos;
}

std::size_t find_delimiter_position(hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer, std::byte delimiter) {
    for (std::size_t i = 0; i < buffer.size(); ++i) {
        if (buffer[i] == delimiter) {
            return i;
        }
    }
    return hexi::pmc::buffer_read<std::vector<std::byte>>::npos;
}

bool check_buffer_contains(hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer, std::byte value) {
    return find_byte_in_buffer(buffer, value) != hexi::pmc::buffer_read<std::vector<std::byte>>::npos;
}
```</answer>

    buffer.skip(header.body_length);
    return header;
}
```</answer>

#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <string>
#include <string_view>

void write_string_prefixed_varint(hexi::binary_stream& stream, const std::string& str) {
    stream << hexi::prefixed_varint(str);
}

std::string read_string_prefixed_varint(hexi::binary_stream& stream) {
    std::string temp;
    stream >> hexi::prefixed_varint(temp);
    return temp;
}

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/shared.h>
#include <array>
#include <cstdint>
#include <algorithm>

using namespace hexi::detail;

template<std::size_t Size>
constexpr auto create_filled_array(std::uint8_t value) {
    return []<std::size_t... I>(std::index_sequence<I...>) {
        return std::array<std::uint8_t, Size>{((void)I, value)...};
    }(std::make_index_sequence<Size>{});
}

bool verify_filled_array(const auto& arr, std::uint8_t expected_value) {
    return std::all_of(arr.begin(), arr.end(), 
                      [expected_value](auto element) {
                          return element == expected_value;
                      });
}
```

**Explanation:**

1. **`create_filled_array`**:
   - Uses a lambda with `std::index_sequence` to generate a parameter pack of indices.
   - The parameter pack `((void)I, value)...` ensures each array element is set to `value`, discarding the index `I` to avoid unused variable warnings.
   - Returns a `constexpr` `std::array` filled with the specified value at compile time.

2. **`verify_filled_array`**:
   - Uses `std::all_of` to check if all elements in the container match `expected_value`.
   - Works with any iterable container (e.g., `std::array`, `std::vector`) due to the `auto` parameter.</answer>

#include <vector>
#include <string>

struct CompactPacket {
    uint32_t packet_id;
    std::vector<std::string> tags;
    std::string data;
};

std::vector<uint8_t> serialize_compact_packet(const CompactPacket& packet) {
    std::vector<uint8_t> buffer;
    hexi::stream_adaptor adaptor(buffer);
    
    // Serialize packet_id as uint32_t

    adaptor << hexi::prefixed_varint(packet.tags);
    
    // Serialize data string using prefixed_varint

    packet.data = adaptor.get<std::string>(hexi::prefixed_varint<std::string>());
    
    return packet;
}

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <string>
#include <cstdint>
#include <vector>
#include <algorithm>

struct PaddedData {
    uint16_t header;
    std::string data;
    uint32_t footer;
};

std::vector<uint8_t> serialize_with_padding(const PaddedData& packet) {
    std::vector<uint8_t> buffer;
    
    // Reserve space for efficiency
    buffer.reserve(sizeof(packet.header) + sizeof(uint16_t) + packet.data.size() + 16 + sizeof(packet.footer));
    
    // Write header
    const uint8_t* header_bytes = reinterpret_cast<const uint8_t*>(&packet.header);
    buffer.insert(buffer.end(), header_bytes, header_bytes + sizeof(packet.header));
    
    // Write data length (uint16_t)
    uint16_t data_len = static_cast<uint16_t>(packet.data.size());
    const uint8_t* len_bytes = reinterpret_cast<const uint8_t*>(&data_len);
    buffer.insert(buffer.end(), len_bytes, len_bytes + sizeof(data_len));
    
    // Write data string
    buffer.insert(buffer.end(), packet.data.begin(), packet.data.end());
    
    // Write 16 bytes of padding (0x00) using fill<16>(0x00)
    auto padding = hexi::fill<16>(0x00);
    buffer.insert(buffer.end(), padding.begin(), padding.end());
    
    // Write footer
    const uint8_t* footer_bytes = reinterpret_cast<const uint8_t*>(&packet.footer);
    buffer.insert(buffer.end(), footer_bytes, footer_bytes + sizeof(packet.footer));
    
    return buffer;
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/allocators/block_allocator.h>
#include <vector>
#include <memory>

struct PoolObject {
    int id;
    double value;
    
    PoolObject() : id(0), value(0.0) {}
    PoolObject(int i, double v) : id(i), value(v) {}
};

std::vector<PoolObject*> create_object_pool(hexi::block_allocator<PoolObject, 10>& allocator) {
    std::vector<PoolObject*> objects;
    auto* chunk = allocator.allocate(3);
    
    for (int i = 0; i < 3; ++i) {
        new (chunk + i) PoolObject(i + 1, static_cast<double>(i + 1) * 1.5);
        objects.push_back(chunk + i);
    }
    
    return objects;
}
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_base.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstddef>

class CustomBuffer : public hexi::pmc::buffer_base {
    std::vector<std::byte> data_;
    
public:
    std::size_t size() const override {
        return data_.size();
    }
    
    bool empty() const override {
        return data_.empty();
    }
    
    // Helper method to add data (for testing)
    void add_data(const void* source, std::size_t length) {
        const std::byte* bytes = static_cast<const std::byte*>(source);
        data_.insert(data_.end(), bytes, bytes + length);
    }
    
    // Helper method to clear data (for testing)
    void clear() {
        data_.clear();
    }
};
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_read_adaptor.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_write_adaptor.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

std::vector<uint8_t> write_and_read_data(const std::vector<uint8_t>& input_data) {
    std::vector<uint8_t> buffer;
    hexi::pmc::buffer_write_adaptor adaptor(buffer);
    
    // Check available space before writing
    auto free_space = adaptor.free();
    
    // Write data to the buffer
    adaptor.write(input_data);
    
    // Create a read adaptor from the same buffer
    hexi::pmc::buffer_read_adaptor read_adaptor(buffer);
    
    // Read the data back
    std::vector<uint8_t> output;
    output.resize(read_adaptor.size());
    read_adaptor.read(output.data(), output.size());
    
    return output;
}

std::vector<uint8_t> peek_data_without_advancing(const std::vector<uint8_t>& buffer_data, std::size_t peek_size) {
    hexi::pmc::buffer_read_adaptor read_adaptor(buffer_data);
    
    // Peek at data without advancing the read pointer
    std::vector<uint8_t> peek_buffer(peek_size);
    read_adaptor.copy(peek_buffer.data(), peek_size);
    
    // Get pointer to the current read position
    const uint8_t* ptr = read_adaptor.read_ptr();
    
    // Consume the peeked data
    std::vector<uint8_t> result(peek_size);
    read_adaptor.read(result.data(), peek_size);
    
    return result;
}
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_read_adaptor.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>
#include <cstring>

struct Header {
    uint32_t magic;
    uint16_t version;
};

Header peek_header_without_reading(const std::vector<uint8_t>& buffer) {
    hexi::buffer_read_adaptor adaptor(buffer);
    
    // Peek at the header data without consuming it
    auto header_data = adaptor.copy(6);
    
    // Get pointer to current position (should be start of buffer)
    auto ptr = adaptor.read_ptr();
    
    // Construct Header from the copied data
    Header header;
    std::memcpy(&header.magic, header_data.data(), sizeof(header.magic));
    std::memcpy(&header.version, header_data.data() + 4, sizeof(header.version));
    
    return header;
}

std::vector<uint8_t> peek_then_conditionally_read(const std::vector<uint8_t>& buffer) {
    hexi::buffer_read_adaptor adaptor(buffer);
    
    // Peek at the first byte
    auto first_byte = adaptor.copy(1);
    
    // Conditionally read more data based on the first byte
    if (first_byte[0] == 0x01) {
        // Read the entire buffer if first byte is 0x01
        return adaptor.read();
    } else {
        // Return empty vector if condition not met
        return {};
    }
}
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <hexi/endian.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>
#include <span>

struct ProtocolPacket {
    uint32_t message_id;
    std::vector<uint16_t> data_values;
    uint32_t checksum;
};

std::vector<uint8_t> serialize_protocol_packet(const ProtocolPacket& packet) {
    hexi::binary_stream stream;
    
    // Convert message_id to big-endian and write
    auto converted_id = hexi::endian::native_to_big(packet.message_id);
    stream.write(converted_id);
    
    // Convert each data_value to big-endian and write as container
    std::vector<uint16_t> converted_data;
    for (auto val : packet.data_values) {
        converted_data.push_back(hexi::endian::native_to_big(val));
    }
    stream.write_container(converted_data);
    
    // Convert checksum to big-endian and write
    auto converted_checksum = hexi::endian::native_to_big(packet.checksum);
    stream.write(converted_checksum);
    
    return stream.to_vector();
}

ProtocolPacket deserialize_protocol_packet(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    hexi::binary_stream stream(buffer);
    ProtocolPacket packet;
    
    // Read message_id and convert from big-endian to native
    uint32_t be_id;
    stream.read(be_id);
    packet.message_id = hexi::endian::big_to_native(be_id);
    
    // Read count of data_values (converted from big-endian to native)
    uint32_t count_be;
    stream.read(count_be);
    uint32_t count = hexi::endian::big_to_native(count_be);
    
    // Read data_values vector, converting each element from big-endian to native
    std::vector<uint16_t> data;
    for (uint32_t i = 0; i < count; ++i) {
        uint16_t value_be;
        stream.read(value_be);
        data.push_back(hexi::endian::big_to_native(value_be));
    }
    packet.data_values = std::move(data);
    
    // Read checksum and convert from big-endian to native
    uint32_t be_checksum;
    stream.read(be_checksum);
    packet.checksum = hexi::endian::big_to_native(be_checksum);
    
    return packet;
}

struct ProtocolHeader {
    uint32_t message_id;
};

ProtocolHeader extract_protocol_header(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    // Get the first 4 bytes as a span
    auto header_bytes = buffer.subspan(0, 4);
    
    // Convert from big-endian to native
    uint32_t message_id = hexi::endian::big_to_native(
        *reinterpret_cast<const uint32_t*>(header_bytes.data()));
    
    return {message_id};
}
```</answer>

struct MeasurementData {
    uint32_t sensor_id;
    std::vector<float> temperatures;
    double pressure;
    
    void serialise(auto& adaptor) const {
        // Convert sensor_id to little-endian
        auto converted_id = hexi::conditional_reverse(sensor_id, std::endian::native, std::endian::little);
        adaptor.write(&converted_id, sizeof(converted_id));
        
        // Write each temperature converted to little-endian
        for (float temp : temperatures) {
            auto converted_temp = hexi::conditional_reverse(temp, std::endian::native, std::endian::little);
            adaptor.write(&converted_temp, sizeof(converted_temp));
        }
        
        // Convert pressure to little-endian
        auto converted_pressure = hexi::conditional_reverse(pressure, std::endian::native, std::endian::little);
        adaptor.write(&converted_pressure, sizeof(converted_pressure));
    }
    
    void deserialise(auto& adaptor) {
        // Read sensor_id (will be little-endian)
        uint32_t raw_id;
        adaptor.read(&raw_id, sizeof(raw_id));
        sensor_id = hexi::little_to_native_inplace(raw_id);
        
        // Read temperatures
        temperatures.clear();
        float temp;
        while (adaptor.read(&temp, sizeof(temp))) {
            temperatures.push_back(hexi::little_to_native_inplace(temp));
        }
        
        // Read pressure

    if (null_pos == std::string_view::npos) {
        return {0, ""};
    }
    
    std::string_view sensor_name(name_view.substr(0, null_pos));
    
    // Enforce read bounds for sensor_id

    // Read sensor_id (little-endian)

    // Skip the rest of the data
    hexi::skip(buffer.data() + null_pos + 5, buffer.size() - (null_pos + 5));
    
    return {sensor_id, sensor_name};
}
